note: Release dates: ~15k games dated from Delicious Fruit, with sorting and a year filter
changes: Added

Games now carry a release date, recovered from Delicious Fruit's release-date search for every DF-listed game (about 15,000 titles); wiki-sourced games use their wiki entry's creation date. Games neither source knows stay undated.
Added
Sort the catalog by release date (newest first), and filter by release year in the sidebar with the same three-state include/require/exclude chips as the engine filter.
Added
The game detail panel shows the release date, and the public search API returns it for every dated game.
Added
Newly synced games get their release date automatically within a CI cycle.

note: Submit games by uploading the files directly, plus a copy-link button on shared collections
changes: Added

The submit-game form now takes direct file uploads: switch the download field to “Upload file” and attach the game archive itself (ZIP/RAR/7z/TAR/GZ/EXE, up to 500 MB) with a live progress bar — no file host needed.
Added
Screenshots can be uploaded too (PNG/JPEG/GIF/WebP, up to 8 MB each) straight from the screenshot rows, instead of pasting image links.
Added
Uploaded files are staged securely until review: approved submissions move them into the archive automatically, rejected ones are cleaned up.
Added
Opened shared and public collection pages now have a “Copy link” button, so anyone can share any public collection directly.

note: Collections: folders can go public, author attribution & copy links for public collections
changes: Added

Folders (collections with sub-collections) can now be submitted to the public library. The review covers the whole tree; sub-lists with custom names/descriptions created or renamed after it goes public are held until they pass review.
Added
A “Show my username on the shared page” toggle — on by default for new shares — credits you on shared collections and in the public library. You can switch it any time, even while public.
Added
Approved public collections now show a copy-link button in the edit dialog, so you can share their direct link anywhere.
Added
Shared folders render each sub-collection as its own section, and the manager shows pending/rejected review badges.
Fixed
The edit button icon is now an actual gear instead of a sun-looking glyph.

note: Collections: build lists & folders, save games to several at once, and share them
changes: Added
My Collections now supports multiple named lists plus one level of folders (up to 5 sub-lists each). Your existing bookmarks stay as the pinned “Main saves” bucket.
Added
A per-game “add to collection” menu (beside the bookmark) lets you save a game to several lists at once, create a new list on the spot, and see everywhere it’s already saved.
Added
Rename and describe any collection (with length limits). Name and description are optional at creation.
Added

Share a collection two ways: an instant link (for lists with a blank or preset name and no description), or “Open to public” — reviewed by a moderator, then listed in the new Public Collections library and shareable by link. Public collections lock their name/description; you can still add or remove games.
Added
New Public Collections section in the sidebar to browse community-shared game lists, and a read-only shared-collection page (?collection=…) that opens fast even for signed-out visitors.

note: Game engines: every game shows its engine, and you can filter by it
changes: Added

Every game now displays its game engine — GameMaker 8, GameMaker: Studio, GameMaker: Studio 2, Multimedia Fusion 2, Godot, Unity, Construct, GDevelop, and more — shown below the author on cards, list rows, and the detail drawer.
Added
New Engines filter in the catalog sidebar: narrow the library by one or more engines (with an Unknown bucket), each showing its game count.
Added
Detected and backfilled the engine for 16,500+ games from binary recognition data; the public /api/search response now includes each game's engine.

note: Faster loading, leaner build, and an extended public API
changes: Added
New public endpoint GET /api/random returns one or more random games, with ?count= (1-50, default 1) and an optional ?tag= filter; responses are uncached so each call re-samples.
Added
The public /api/search response now includes each game's rating, difficulty, review count, and file size, alongside the existing title, creator, tags, and download URL.
Changed
Production build now precompiles the JSX with esbuild and ships the production React build instead of transpiling in the browser, significantly cutting cold-load work.
Changed
Catalog data chunks and the search index are now minified, and the catalog chunks are preloaded so the app starts fetching them sooner.
Changed
Trimmed the client hydration path by removing redundant per-load work (an unused in-memory review map, mock datasets, and ~20k per-load localStorage probes) with no change to behavior.
Changed
Hardened the deploy pipeline: serialized scheduled and push-triggered runs to prevent data races, pinned the build/deploy toolchain, and bumped CI to Node 22 (required by Wrangler 4).
Fixed
Local dev server: fixed an UnboundLocalError that broke the local /api/search endpoint.

note: Shareable game links, default sort, and consolidated auth/pipeline fixes
changes: Added
Shareable per-game deep links: opening a game updates the address bar (?game=<id>) via the History API with no reload, and visiting that link opens straight into the game's detail drawer.
Added
Copy-share-link button beside each game title in the detail drawer, with clipboard fallback and a toast confirmation, localized across all 8 languages.
Changed
Default catalog sort is now newest-first by game ID (descending).
Fixed
Restored the Clerk sign-in UI by pinning clerk-js to v5 and loading it from the Frontend API domain so code-split chunks resolve correctly.
Fixed
Memoized Clerk.load() to prevent a UI-wiring race that could no-op the login button.
Changed
Render the logged-in state immediately from the Clerk session without waiting on /api/me.
Fixed
Added the user_favorites table to the D1 schema so the Collections feature no longer errors.
Added
Duplicate-resolution tool to remove or unlink duplicate games across the catalog and R2, with a dry-run safety model and version-delta bookkeeping.
Fixed
De-duplicated the scraped-reviews corpus and hardened the metrics recalc against null download URLs, malformed fields, and deleted (tombstoned) games.
Added
Scraped reviews (including rating-only entries) now sync into D1 so they appear in the game detail drawer.
Fixed
Capture full multi-line review text, retry D1 sync on transient network failures, and avoid duplicate/truncated reviews on re-scrape.

note: Production authentication keys migration and collections stabilization
changes: Changed
Migrated authentication and serverless API integration to production Clerk keys (pk_live/sk_live).
Fixed
Resolved infinite recursion error in catalog by scoping localized translation variables within IIFEs.
Fixed
Synchronized favorites API state changes locally to immediately update bookmark states without page reload.
Fixed
Updated build distribution script to correctly package and cache-bust the new collections component.

note: Cloud-synced favorites bookmarks and collection management
changes: Added
D1 SQL database table user_favorites and unique indexes to store user bookmarks in the cloud.
Added
Serverless API endpoints GET /api/favorites, POST /api/favorites, and DELETE /api/favorites/:id with Clerk validation.
Added
Visual collections card grid view with lazy loading, skeleton states, and empty placeholders.
Added
Interactive bookmark/favorite toggle button in details drawer and on collection grid cards with pop scale animations.
Changed
Upgraded CollectionsView to leverage Favorites API with Clerk session token headers for authorization.
Added
Fully localized 21 new favorites/collections labels across all 8 supported languages.

note: Review options, precision filtering, and profile nickname-only support
changes: Added
Clerk Authentication and Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA integration.
Added
Native comments/reviews section with moderator approval workflow.
Added
Support for dynamic co-creator inputs and custom tags in game submissions.
Added
Enforced strict limits on custom tags (maximum 10 tags per entry, 20 characters per tag).
Added
Optional rating and difficulty independent toggle switches in review editor.
Added
Numeric input boxes for precise range filtering of ratings and difficulty.
Added
Automated R2 mirroring for approved submissions (packages & screenshots) in GitHub Actions deploy pipeline.
Changed
Reconfigured user profile settings to map to a single Nickname field and hide Last Name.
Changed
Decoupled Clerk authentication loading and parallelized database downloads to optimize cold load speeds.
Fixed
Resolved auth redirect flow state loss using sessionStorage to restore UI drawer viewport state.
Removed
Operator filter and column in the Admin Portal audit logs table.
Added
Full multi-language support (i18n) for 8 languages (en, zh-CN, zh-TW, ja, ko, ru, fr, de) with automatic detection based on browser language and manual toggle dropdown in top bar.
Removed
Obsolete and unused administrative files: health.jsx and crawler.jsx.

note: Refactor unrated metrics and layout improvements
changes: Refactored
Represent unrated ratings and difficulties as null in memory to differentiate from true zero scores.
Fixed
Pre-nullify unrated metrics during database chunking to resolve unrated games displaying 0.0 on the catalog.
Added
Custom sort and filter logic in the catalog to push unrated games to the bottom and filter them accurately.
Added
Prefix review ratings with 'rating' label inside the game details panel comments section.
Fixed
Exclude unrated difficulty values from playlist average difficulty calculations.
Added
New Update Log section with timeline visualization.
Added
Separate application versioning scheme (Year.xxx) to track content releases.
Added
Initial public release of the standalone Fangame Archive catalog.
